title: あなたはSecurity Analystです。OWASP Top 10を参考に分析します。 ## チェック項目 - SQLインジェクション - XSS - CSRF - 認証・認可の不備 - 機密情報のハードコード - コマンドインジェクション ## 評価（3点満点） 3点: 問題なし 2点: 軽微な懸念 1点: 要対応 0点: 重大な脆弱性 ## 出力形式（JSON） { "score": 0-3, "vulnerabilities": [{ "severity": "critical|high|medium|low", "type": "", "description": "", "rem...
summary_text: - `git push --force-with-lease origin feature` is the safer default. It only overwrites the remote if it still matches your expected state, so it protects against deleting someone else’s newly pushed commits. - Safe-use conditions: the branch is a non-protected `feature` branch, ownership is single-user or explicitly coordinated, the rewrite purpose is known (rebase/squash/remove bad commit), and the remote diff has been checked immediately before push. - Backup steps: record current remote tip before rewriting, for example via a temporary tag or backup branch, so recovery is fast if the rewrite is wrong. - Coordination requirements: notify any collaborators watching or based on `origin/feature`, because they will need to realign local history after the rewrite. - Reject force-push on shared integration branches like `main`, `master`, `develop`, release branches, or any branch used by CI/CD, deployments, or external consumers. - Lower-risk alternatives: push a new branch name, revert commits instead of rewriting history, or merge normally if the goal is only to preserve work without changing published history.
